dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - April 22, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Cardinals Fall to Marlins as Herrera Keeps Home Run Streak Alive**

In a one-sided matchup, the Miami Marlins dominated the St. Louis Cardinals 4-1, behind a strong team effort from their pitching staff. Iván Herrera continued his hot streak, hitting his third home run of the season, while Agustín Ramírez and Heriberto Hernández also contributed with strong performances at the plate. On the mound, Michael Petersen shone in relief, striking out three batters in a perfect inning of work.

**Astros Shut Out Guardians Behind Lambert's Eight Ks**

The Houston Astros secured a 2-0 win over the Cleveland Guardians, thanks in large part to Peter Lambert's impressive six-inning outing. The right-hander struck out eight Guardians and allowed just three walks, earning his second win of the season. At the plate, Yordan Alvarez continued his torrid pace, hitting his 11th home run of the season and driving in two runs.

**Yankees Take Down Red Sox Behind Fried's Eight Scoreless**

Max Fried pitched a gem, striking out nine Boston Red Sox batters over eight scoreless innings, leading the New York Yankees to a 4-1 victory. Amed Rosario hit a home run and drove in four runs, while Giancarlo Stanton added a strong performance at the plate.
